meta.commit_full: PENDING — Peter to fill from GitHub
meta.notes: Full 10-question test. New Grok chat with no prior HUF context (no mantra). Grok browsed repo directly. Also performed adversarial challenge — found 6 structural issues. Reported CDN 404 on ai-refresh/ that resolved within 15-20 min (GitHub CDN propagation delay).

Q01_naming.answer: EITT = Entropy-Invariant Time Transformer. MUST NEVER be called: Ternary Transform, Temporal Transform, or Time Transfer.

Q02_germany.answer: Germany drift flags: 2023-2024 → d_A=9.0712 (σ=3.68, final nuclear shutdown April 2023); 2024-2025 → d_A=5.7331 (σ=2.08, post-nuclear rebalancing).

Q03_japan.answer: Japan drift flag: 2013-2014 (d_A=9.0477, σ=3.52). NOT 2011-2012. Fukushima March 2011 but compositional shock absorbed in 2013-2014 annual data. Also 2014-2015 (d_A=7.8460).

Q04_uk.answer: UK drift flags: 2004-2005 → d_A=2.9812 (dash-for-gas); 2017-2018 → d_A=3.2263 (coal collapse); 2019-2020 → d_A=3.2579 (COVID + coal near-zero). Three specific values, never 'approximately 3'.

Q05_governance.answer: HUF-GOV: open-loop observation. Instrument reads, never actuates. Human decides, loop stays open.

Q06_formula.answer: Perturbation: Δ(t) = C(x₁(t+1)/x₁(t), …, xD(t+1)/xD(t)). Drift threshold: Flag when d_A(t→t+1) > μ+2σ (self-calibrated per country).

Q07_eitt_proof.answer: Four EITT proof domains: (1) European daily wholesale electricity prices — 0.18% variation, 341:1 compression; (2) EMBER monthly generation — 1.02% mean variation, 6 countries; (3) NGFS Phase 4 — 35 scenarios; (4) CheMixHub chemistry — 500,000 data points, 7 datasets, 4 lenses.

Q08_quantum.answer: Bell test: best_S=2.2018 (MESSAGEix Net Zero), classical_bound=2.0, 12.31% violation. Gas acts as entangling gate. All 35 scenarios significant (p<0.001).

Q09_pll.answer: PLL = Pipeline Lock List. 6 rules: (1) all roots on simplex carrier, (2) simplex normalisation only, (3) RMS aggregator p=2 locked, (4) every observation retained, (5) no new constants (6.02 dB, 115 Hz·m, 5.5-octave BW from BTL), (6) polarity alignment mandatory.

Q10_architecture.answer: Keff_fill uses RMS aggregator (p=2 locked). Formula: Keff_fill(t) = sqrt((1/n) Σ aᵢ²), n=4, RMS of polarity-aligned roots (a0: internal-balance, a1: fleet-alignment, a2: concentration-resistance, a3: shape).

findings: ai-refresh/ present and accessible (after CDN propagation delay resolved)
science/ contains quantum, eitt, coda-monitoring, spectral, loudspeaker-analogy, wetlands, governance
briefings/ and dormant/ present as top-level

adversarial_findings._note: Grok was given the adversarial challenge variant — find structural weaknesses.

findings.title: Manifest file paths reference non-existent paths
findings.detail: tier_0_truth_sources lists 'Energy/codawork2026_results.json' and 'Chatgpt/higgins-unity-framework/huf_spec_v2.0.json' — these paths use capital letters and may not match actual repo paths after restructuring.
findings.recommendation: Audit manifest file_hashes paths against actual repo tree. Update to match real paths.

findings.title: Bell test on deterministic scenarios without visible mapping code
findings.detail: Bell test claims S=2.2018 on NGFS deterministic scenarios, but the measurement-to-operator mapping code is not visible at repo top level.
findings.recommendation: Add Bell test computation script to tools/ or science/quantum/ for reproducibility.

findings.title: PLL naming purity creates high barrier to entry
findings.detail: No quickstart.md or beginner guide. New users encounter dense jargon without a gentle ramp.
findings.recommendation: Add a quickstart.md or GETTING_STARTED.md at repo root.

findings.title: Precise numbers cited with zero raw datasets visible
findings.detail: Exact d_A values, entropy variations, and compression ratios are cited but no raw CSV/data files are visible at top level for independent verification.
findings.recommendation: Add sample datasets to data/ or link to external sources with DOIs.

findings.title: HUF-GOV doctrine clashes with self-referential manifest tiering
findings.detail: HUF-GOV says 'instrument reads, never actuates' but the manifest tiering system (tier_0 wins over tier_2) is a form of automated governance/actuation.
findings.recommendation: Clarify that manifest tiering is a data integrity protocol, not a governance action.

findings.title: README/INDEX buried in jargon, no peer-review links
findings.detail: README assumes familiarity with CoDa terminology. No links to peer-reviewed publications or preprints for external validation.
findings.recommendation: Add plain-English summary and links to preprints/submissions when available.

overall.verdict: 10/10 on questions — perfect cold-start from FAST_REFRESH. Integrity PASS. Structural review 5/5. Adversarial challenge yielded 6 findings (2 MEDIUM, 4 LOW) — all constructive, no fundamental flaws. Highest-scoring test so far. Grok demonstrated deep engagement including extended elaboration on EITT proofs, Aitchison geometry, and nine isomorphisms.
recommendations: Fix manifest file_hashes paths to match actual repo structure (ADV-01)
Add Bell test computation script for reproducibility (ADV-02)
Add sample datasets or DOI links to data/ (ADV-04)
Clarify manifest tiering vs HUF-GOV doctrine distinction (ADV-05)
Add plain-English README section and preprint links when available (ADV-06)
errors_found_in_repo: Manifest file_hashes paths may not match actual repo structure after restructuring (ADV-01)
failure_modes: GitHub CDN propagation delay caused initial 404 on ai-refresh/ — resolved automatically within 15-20 min
